basis: computed ceiling: certified final minus clerk-quantified not-yet-counted ballots, snapshot is Friday not election night
evidence: Madera Tribune Nov 7 2014 (docref news/15175914B6DDC6C0): Clerk Rebecca Martinez quantifies 4,385 late VBM + 973 provisional = 5,358 ballots 'left to be counted'; 27,370-5,358=22,012 (80.42%), self-consistent with the row's own prior-known Friday 2:14pm capture of 26,449 (26,449-22,012=4,437, close to the 4,385 late-VBM batch). CORRECTION (2026-07-09): the 5,358-remaining figure is stated as of Friday Nov 7, not election night; any interim processing between Tuesday night and Friday beyond the two named categories can only make the true election-night count LOWER than 22,012, so 22,012 is a CEILING (comparable=false), not a floor as previously labeled.

basis: cited corroboration documents a Wednesday-morning update between the plateau and the derived figure; value retained as a documented ceiling, comparable=false

basis: county clerk's own election-night figure, bracketed unchanged by the county's own posting schedule (next update days later)
evidence: Fresno Bee Nov 8 2012 quotes Clerk Brandi Orth: 'So far, about 160,400 ballots have been counted, putting voter turnout at 39.1%' (matches 160,400/410,188 registered exactly); the Nov 10 2012 companion item states 'Fresno County on Friday did its first vote-count update since Tuesday's election', proving no interim release moved the count between election night and Friday. 160,400/261,652 = 61.30%.

basis: certified-minus-outstanding derivation self-describing as through election night; downgraded from CONFIRMED on 2026-07-09 review because the Nov 6/Nov 8 cross-article reconciliation (~42,600 vs ~22,000+20,400=~42,400) is an arithmetic coincidence between two independently-approximated figures, not an explicit county-schedule-bracketing statement of the kind that supports CONFIRMED elsewhere in this dataset (contrast 2012's 'first vote-count update since Tuesday's election' and 2018's 'will not announce updated vote totals until Friday afternoon'). comparable remains true: the companion Nov 8 article independently confirms the cited 736-vote Costa-Tacherra lead was itself 'on election night,' i.e. the Thursday-quoted state (and its accompanying ~42,600 figure) is the frozen Tuesday-night/Wednesday-morning plateau, not a later Thursday development.
evidence: Fresno Bee Nov 6 2014 'Digesting the results': 'With the Election Day ballot counting finished... approximately 42,600 Fresno County mail and provisional ballots left to count.' This figure is NOT attributed to officials (unlike the same article's adjacent Merced County figure, 'there are 5,757 mail and around 1,500 provisional ballots left to count, officials said late Wednesday'), so it may be a reporter approximation rather than a stated official count. The Nov 8 2014 companion 'Soria takes council race lead' shows no county update between the Wednesday-morning post and Friday 3pm (confirmed by its own statement that Tacherra's '736 on election night' lead only moved to 1,775 via Friday's Madera/Fresno/Merced updates), and offers a same-ballpark but not matching remaining-ballot pool (more than 22,000 processed by Friday plus 10,300+10,100 still remaining = ~42,400, close to but not identical to the Thursday figure's ~42,600). 163,420 minus ~42,600 = 120,820 (73.93%); cross-checked against San Diego's own CONFIRMED 2014 row (73.54%, same election).

basis: certified-minus-outstanding derivation self-describing as the state Wednesday morning after election night, bracketed unchanged by the county's own posting schedule AND (added 2026-07-09) by an independent companion article confirming the promised Friday update was the first update after election night
evidence: Fresno Bee Nov 8 2018 'It's all over but the counting': 'Election Day is over, but there are still 100,000 vote-by-mail and provisional ballots left to count in Fresno County. Registrar Brandi Orth said Wednesday morning that the process of counting about 76,000 mail-in ballots had begun' and 'Fresno County will not announce updated vote totals until Friday afternoon.' CORRECTION (2026-07-09): the promise was independently confirmed kept by Fresno Bee Nov 11 2018 'Election 2018 - Hurtado increases lead, Janz closes slightly on Nunes' (docref news/16FA94EDA82A6CC8): 'About 23,000 of the 100,000 remaining ballots were counted and included in a Friday update from the Fresno County Clerk's office. The next update on the remaining 77,000 votes will come Wednesday at 3 p.m.' (100,000-23,000=77,000, consistent), i.e. no county release moved the count between the Wednesday-morning 100,000-outstanding statement and Friday's update, matching the 2012 bracketing pattern with a genuinely separate article. 256,972 minus 100,000 = 156,972 (61.09%); consistent with this row's already-documented 2018-11-21 canvass snapshot (239,032) as an intermediate point toward certification.

basis: certified-minus-outstanding derivation, corroborated by two independently-bylined articles, bracketed by the registrar's own posting schedule
evidence: 'Buster facing defeat' (news/14270915258D4BE8) and 'Bono Mack, Baca ousters hit clout' (news/14270914E8F945E8), both Nov 8 2012 Press-Enterprise: both state 'with all precincts reporting' and 'about 183,000 vote-by-mail, provisional and damaged ballots remained to be counted countywide'; the registrar's next update is explicitly scheduled for 6 p.m. Thursday Nov 8, and Buster 'didn't concede Wednesday, Nov. 7', confirming no update moved the count between election night and the Wednesday print deadline (San Diego 2012 pattern). 669,627 minus 183,000 = 486,627 (72.67%).
